metadata.name: n8n-node-inventory
metadata.description: Inventario estructurado de nodos y capacidades de n8n para apoyar la skill n8n-workflow-engineering con criterio node-first.
metadata.verification_scope: Verificado mediante inspección de workflows existentes vía n8n MCP/API. No equivale a catálogo global de nodos instalados.

verification_policy.verified-instance: Confirmado en workflows reales o catálogo real de la instancia de n8n usada por Operaciones.
verification_policy.verified-docs: Confirmado contra documentación oficial de la versión correspondiente.
verification_policy.known-general: Conocimiento general del ecosistema n8n, pendiente de verificar en la instancia real.
verification_policy.candidate: Nodo o capacidad plausible que debe buscarse en la instancia antes de diseñar.
verification_policy.verified-design: Capacidad validada a nivel de diseño interno o arquitectura, aunque no necesariamente como nodo nativo.
verification_policy.deprecated-or-unknown: Nodo dudoso, renombrado, desactualizado o no confirmado.

metadata.node_first_rule: Before using Code or HTTP Request, check whether a native n8n node, AI node, database node, integration node, or visual pattern can solve the need more safely and maintainably.

permissions_observed: get_execution: endpoint exposed but not used
create_draft_workflow: endpoint exposed but not used
update_inactive_workflow: endpoint exposed but not used
limitations: No n8n version endpoint was exposed by the current MCP/API.
No installed-node-catalog endpoint was exposed by the current MCP/API.
No installed community package registry was exposed by the current MCP/API.
Inventory is based on nodes verified inside existing workflows, not on a global installed-node registry.
Absence of a node in this inventory does not prove it is not installed.
notes: No workflow was activated or deactivated.
Sensitive values were observed in workflow parameters and are intentionally omitted from this inventory.

nodes.name: Webhook
nodes.recommended_use: Receive external events or internal HTTP callbacks.
prefer_over: Polling when event-driven execution is possible
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use dedicated API/MCP service when authentication, rate limiting or routing exceed n8n requirements.
limitations: Verified only through existing workflow definition

nodes.name: Edit Fields / Set
nodes.recommended_use: Create, select, rename or normalize simple fields visibly.
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Code for dynamic schemas, recursive structures, hashing, chunking or advanced validation.
limitations: Sensitive values should not be stored directly in Set nodes
nodes.observations: Used for Chatwoot field extraction and pipeline configuration.

nodes.name: IF
nodes.recommended_use: Boolean branching based on status, flags, existence or simple comparisons.
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Code when condition logic is multi-step, computed across arrays or not maintainable in expressions.
nodes.observations: Used to determine whether an incoming message should be processed.

nodes.name: AI Agent
nodes.recommended_use: Build conversational agents that can use model, memory and retrieval/tool nodes.
prefer_over: Custom Code orchestration for standard agent flows
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use custom service/MCP when tool governance, state, auth or response control exceed the node capabilities.
limitations: Depends on connected model, memory and tool nodes
nodes.observations: Observed in RAG Chatwoot omnichannel workflow.

nodes.name: OpenAI Chat Model
nodes.recommended_use: Provide OpenAI chat model capability to AI Agent or LangChain workflows.
prefer_over: HTTP Request for supported OpenAI chat model calls
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use HTTP Request when the installed node does not support the required endpoint, model, response format or payload.
limitations: Credential references were not exposed by inspect endpoint

nodes.name: Embeddings OpenAI
nodes.recommended_use: Generate semantic vectors for RAG pipelines.
prefer_over: HTTP Request for embeddings when the node supports the selected model and dimensions
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use HTTP Request to embeddings endpoint if model/dimensions are unsupported by installed node.
limitations: Credential references were not exposed by inspect endpoint

nodes.name: Supabase Vector Store
nodes.recommended_use: Use Supabase/pgvector as RAG retrieval tool for AI Agent workflows.
prefer_over: Manual HTTP/SQL vector retrieval when native tool covers schema and operation
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use PostgreSQL SQL or Supabase RPC/REST for custom pgvector functions, metadata filters or strict schema control.
limitations: Credential references were not exposed by inspect endpoint
Observed as retrieval tool, not generic table upsert node
nodes.observations: Used as RAG retrieval tool for the AI agent.

nodes.name: Postgres Memory
nodes.recommended_use: Persist agent memory by session/conversation in PostgreSQL.
prefer_over: Buffer memory when persistent conversation history is required
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use custom PostgreSQL schema/API when memory governance or query logic needs full control.
limitations: This confirms PostgreSQL memory capability, not the generic PostgreSQL node
nodes.observations: Used for conversation memory keyed by conversation id.

nodes.name: HTTP Request
nodes.recommended_use: Call REST/RPC APIs, MCP endpoints, custom services or unsupported operations.
prefer_over: Native nodes only when they lack the required operation or request control
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Create a dedicated MCP/API wrapper when authentication, redaction, allowlists or reuse are needed.
limitations: Some workflows store sensitive header/key material in parameters; values omitted
External side effects possible if workflows are executed
nodes.observations: Used for Chatwoot API, GitLab MCP, OpenAI embeddings API and Supabase REST/pgvector upsert.

nodes.name: Chat Trigger
nodes.recommended_use: Test chat/agent workflows interactively before production triggers.
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use real channel trigger/webhook for production traffic.
limitations: Observed in inactive workflow
nodes.observations: Used as testing trigger for the RAG agent workflow.

nodes.name: Simple Memory / Buffer Window Memory
nodes.recommended_use: Keep temporary conversation context during agent execution/tests.
prefer_over: Postgres Memory when persistence is not needed
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Postgres Memory when persistent or auditable memory is required.
limitations: Non-persistent compared with Postgres memory

nodes.name: Code
nodes.recommended_use: Deterministic JavaScript for logic that is not maintainable with visual nodes.
prefer_over: Visual nodes only when the visual solution would be fragile or unreadable
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use external service when dependencies, runtime limits, CPU/memory or security constraints exceed n8n Code node.
limitations: Code nodes can contain sensitive logic or data handling; do not expose full source blindly
nodes.observations: Used heavily in RAG ingestion pipeline.

nodes.name: Manual Trigger
nodes.recommended_use: Manual tests, drafts and controlled validation runs.
prefer_over: Schedule/Webhook during early design and debugging
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Schedule/Webhook once the workflow is production-ready.
limitations: Requires manual execution; no automatic schedule observed
nodes.observations: Used in test and draft sync workflows.

nodes.name: Sticky Note
nodes.recommended_use: Document workflow intent, config, risks and schema directly in the canvas.
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Confluence for stable external documentation.
limitations: Not an executable processing node
Must not contain secrets
nodes.observations: Used to document configuration, flow and Supabase schema.

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger / Cron
nodes.recommended_use: Recurring executions such as syncs, reports, ingestions and scheduled maintenance.
prefer_over: External cron when workflow-native scheduling is enough
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use external scheduler when corporate orchestration, retry policy or deployment constraints require it.
limitations: Not observed in inspected workflows
nodes.observations: Should be checked in the UI/global node catalog before relying on it.

nodes.name: Switch
nodes.recommended_use: Route items by source_type, action, category, status or intent.
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Code when routing rules are dynamically generated or require complex scoring.
limitations: Not observed in inspected workflows
nodes.observations: Should be checked in the UI/global node catalog before relying on it.

nodes.name: PostgreSQL
nodes.recommended_use: Direct SQL operations over PostgreSQL-compatible databases if the node is installed and credentials are approved.
prefer_over: HTTP Request for SQL operations when DB credentials and policies allow direct access
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use Supabase REST/RPC via HTTP Request for API-only access, RLS-aware flows, or custom RPC functions.
limitations: Generic PostgreSQL node was not observed; only Postgres Memory was confirmed
nodes.observations: Do not assume availability until global node catalog/UI confirms it.

nodes.name: Supabase generic node
nodes.recommended_use: Use Supabase operations when the installed node supports required table insert/update/upsert/query behavior.
prefer_over: HTTP Request for simple supported Supabase table operations
nodes.fallback_when_not_enough: Use PostgreSQL node or HTTP Request to REST/RPC endpoints for pgvector, custom match functions, or unsupported upserts.
limitations: Not observed as generic Supabase table node; Supabase Vector Store is verified
nodes.observations: Check UI/global node catalog before designing generic Supabase operations around it.

community_or_custom_nodes.note: No community/custom nodes were observed in inspected workflows, but absence cannot be proven without a global package/node-registry endpoint.

relevant_workflow_patterns.use_case: Receive Chatwoot messages, filter valid incoming messages, answer using an AI agent with Supabase vector retrieval and PostgreSQL memory, then send the answer back through an API call.
relevant_workflow_patterns.notes: Workflow is inactive. External impact nodes exist but were not executed.

relevant_workflow_patterns.use_case: Read knowledge files via an internal GitLab MCP, normalize content, generate chunks and hashes, deduplicate chunks, create OpenAI embeddings and upsert rows into Supabase/pgvector.
relevant_workflow_patterns.notes: Workflow is inactive. Recent manual executions exist. Sensitive config values were observed and omitted. Embeddings OpenAI and Supabase Vector Store are verified in another workflow and should be considered before keeping HTTP Request for those operations.

relevant_workflow_patterns.use_case: Minimal test workflow for MCP draft validation.
relevant_workflow_patterns.notes: Workflow is inactive and marked as test/delete.

recommended_updates_to_catalog: Mark inventory as verified from workflow usage, not from full installed-node registry.
Add limitation: current MCP/API does not expose n8n version.
Add limitation: current MCP/API does not expose global node catalog or installed community package list.
Add confirmed IA/RAG nodes: AI Agent, OpenAI Chat Model, Embeddings OpenAI, Supabase Vector Store, Postgres Memory and Buffer Window Memory.
Add confirmed integration pattern: GitLab via HTTP Request to internal MCP rather than native GitLab node.
Add confirmed ingestion pattern: GitLab MCP -> Code normalization/chunking/hashing/deduplication -> OpenAI embeddings -> Supabase REST/pgvector upsert.
Add security note: avoid storing service-role keys, API tokens or bearer material directly in Set nodes or HTTP headers; migrate to n8n credentials or environment variables.
Add note that no community/custom nodes were observed in inspected workflows, but absence cannot be proven without a package/node-registry endpoint.
pending_work: Obtain real n8n version used by Operations.
Export/list installed node types from the real instance or UI.
Confirm whether generic Supabase, generic PostgreSQL, GitLab, GitHub, Microsoft Teams, Outlook, Google Sheets and Spreadsheet File nodes are installed.
Confirm whether Structured Output Parser, Document Loaders, Text Splitters and additional LangChain nodes are available.
Replace candidate/known-general statuses with verified-instance where possible.
Add operation-level schemas for critical nodes.
Add internal workflow examples once validated.
Remove secrets from workflow parameters and migrate them to n8n credentials or environment variables.
